entry.title: Re: Plan Data Retraction from BPC (on BW/4HANA) to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Good blog,

Question.

Why are you moving the data of the ADSO active table from BPC BW/4 Hana into S/4 Hana. Generally data extraction should be done from S/4 hana to BW/4 hana and do the modelling in native BW/4 HANA.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

I am thrilled to express interest in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative, further strengthening my SAP journey. As a recent Educate to Employ program participant, I've developed a solid SAP foundation.


With a passion for procurement and supply chain management, I've dedicated extensive time to self-study via SAP Learning Hub. However, Nigeria's high certification costs pose challenges.


This initiative offers a valuable opportunity to:


- Validate skills
- Enhance organizational contributions
- Streamline supplier lifecycle management
- Optimize procurement processes
- Drive digital transformation


Thank you for making this program accessible. I'm eager to advance my SAP expertise and leverage this certification for career growth.

Best regards,

Taiwo Sanni

entry.title: Asset Aquisition posting to WBS in S4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi All

I have a requirement, as I want the asset aquisition value should be part of my WBS cost reports. When I create any Asset PO with Account assignment as A, the entire cost goes to Asset and there is no way to post a statisctical entry to WBS. 
In the asset master, I am not able to manually update the Investment WBS in Public cloud as I could have done in Private cloud. 

Kindly advise.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

I am particularly interested in learning SAP Analytics Cloud and believe that the digital skills initiative by SAP Learning is a fantastic opportunity for individuals like myself. I am eager to take on the challenge and undergo the necessary training to achieve SAP Certification.

Thank you for considering my interest in this initiative.

entry.title: Error while generating E-Invoice
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

The customer has issued a Tax Invoice with the Bill To/Ship To Address ID as "S D CORPORATION," but in the BP Master, the Address ID is recorded as "SD CORPORATION."

Upon reviewing the BP Master change log, we did not find any irrelevant entries related to the Address ID. Could you please advise what might be the reason for this discrepancy, and how we can check if the user updated the details from the backend?

According to the BP Master log, the Address ID was updated from "S D CORPORATION" to "SD CORPORATION" at 18:34:33 on 20/09/24, while the invoice was created earlier, at 15:09:13 on the same day.

Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Quiz: incorrect number of selections
entry.content: Hello Margit, even I am getting the same error. Could you please help ? Case 5000031950. Thanks, Minal

Can you please guide, how will I get the opportunity? 

Thank you

Shubhangi

entry.title: RAP additional save
entry.content:
Managed RAP additional save.

Introduction.

We can add external functionality to the managed save sequence by using the extra save. This feature facilitates the reusing of services such as change documents and application logs. The RAP framework defaults to handling managed saves in this way; however, additional steps can be added to the managed save. Here, we use the saver class and the save_modfied method. Updated fields, updated data, and completed operations are all saved when an operation is carried out on an entity's instance. In this instance, the log table will contain any changed data that takes place within an entity instance.

 In this scenario for the data modified in the instance of an entity the changed data will be saved in the log table.

: create the log table to save the data. 

 

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'log data changes table' 

@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 

@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 

@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 

@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 

define table znp_t_log_travel { 

 

key client : abap.clnt not null; 

key change_id : abap.raw(16) not null; 

travelid : /dmo/travel_id not null; 

changing_operation : abap.char(10); 

changed_field_name : abap.char(32); 

changed_value : abap.char(32); 

created_at : timestampl; 

 

}

 

 

 

 

 

 

To do this additional save  is used, to save the data in a managed scenario addition.

  • In the Behavior definition define additional save  
  • For the entity. 
  • It will create one local class and method save modified. In the implementation class. 

 

 
When a new instance is created, the additional data is saved in the entity's database. Log table. 

For to this implement define the saver class and implement the methods in the behavior pool. 

For to save the implemented the save_modified method in the save class. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

METHOD save_modified. 
DATA : lt_travel_log TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF znp_t_log_travel. 
DATA : lt_travel_log_c TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF znp_t_log_travel. 
DATA : lt_travel_log_u TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF znp_t_log_travel. 

 

IF create-znp_i_travel_m IS NOT INITIAL. 
lt_travel_log = CORRESPONDING #( create-znp_i_travel_m ). 

LOOP AT lt_travel_log ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_travel_log>). 
<ls_travel_log>-changing_operation = 'CREATE'. 
GET TIME STAMP FIELD <ls_travel_log>-created_at. 
READ TABLE create-znp_i_travel_m ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_travel>) 
WITH TABLE KEY entity 
COMPONENTS TravelId = <ls_travel_log>-travelid. 

IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL. 

IF <ls_travel>-%control-BookingFee = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed. 

<ls_travel_log>-changed_field_name = 'Booking Fee'. 

<ls_travel_log>-changed_value = <ls_travel>-BookingFee. 

TRY. 

<ls_travel_log>-change_id = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ). 

CATCH cx_uuid_error. 

"handle exception 

ENDTRY. 

APPEND <ls_travel_log> TO lt_travel_log_c. 
ENDIF. 

IF <ls_travel>-%control-OverallStatus = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed. 

<ls_travel_log>-changed_field_name = 'Overall Status'. 

<ls_travel_log>-changed_value = <ls_travel>-OverallStatus. 

TRY. 

<ls_travel_log>-change_id = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ). 

CATCH cx_uuid_error. 

"handle exception 

ENDTRY. 
APPEND <ls_travel_log> TO lt_travel_log_c. 
ENDIF. 
ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 
INSERT znp_t_log_travel FROM TABLE @LT_travel_log_c. 
ENDIF. 

 

 

IF update-znp_i_travel_m IS NOT INITIAL. 
lt_travel_log = CORRESPONDING #( update-znp_i_travel_m ). 

LOOP AT update-znp_i_travel_m ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_log_update>). 
ASSIGN lt_travel_log[ travelid = <ls_log_update>-travelid ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_log_u>). 
<ls_log_u>-changing_operation = 'UPDATE'. 

GET TIME STAMP FIELD <ls_log_u>-created_at. 
IF <ls_log_update>-%control-customerid = if_abap_behv=>mk-on. 
<ls_log_u>-changed_value = <ls_log_update>-customerid. 

TRY. 
<ls_log_u>-change_id = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) . 
CATCH cx_uuid_error. 
ENDTRY. 

<ls_log_u>-changed_field_name = 'customer_id'. 
APPEND <ls_log_u> TO lt_travel_log_u. 
ENDIF. 

IF <ls_log_update>-%control-description = if_abap_behv=>mk-on. 
<ls_log_u>-changed_value = <ls_log_update>-description. 
TRY. 
<ls_log_u>-change_id = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) . 
CATCH cx_uuid_error. 
ENDTRY. 
<ls_log_u>-changed_field_name = 'description'. 
APPEND <ls_log_u> TO lt_travel_log_u. 
ENDIF. 
ENDLOOP. 

INSERT znp_t_log_travel FROM TABLE @LT_travel_log_u. 

ENDIF. 

IF delete-znp_i_travel_m IS NOT INITIAL. 
lt_travel_log = CORRESPONDING #( delete-znp_i_travel_m ). 
LOOP AT lt_travel_log ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_log_del>). 
<ls_log_del>-changing_operation = 'DELETE'. 
GET TIME STAMP FIELD <ls_log_del>-created_at. 
TRY. 
<ls_log_del>-change_id = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) . 
CATCH cx_uuid_error. 

"handle exception 
ENDTRY. 
ENDLOOP. 

INSERT znp_t_log_travel FROM TABLE @LT_travel_log. 
ENDIF. 

 

********************************************************************** 

********************************************************************** 

 

DATA: lt_book_suppl TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF znp_t_booksuppl. 

IF create-znp_i_booksuppl IS NOT INITIAL. 
lt_book_suppl = VALUE #( FOR ls_booksup IN create-znp_i_booksuppl ( 
travel_id = ls_booksup-TravelId 
booking_id = ls_booksup-BookingId 
booking_supplement_id = ls_booksup-BookingSupplementId 
supplement_id = ls_booksup-SupplementId 
price = ls_booksup-Price 
currency_code = ls_booksup-CurrencyCode 
last_changed_at = ls_booksup-LastChangedAt 

) ). 
INSERT znp_t_booksuppl FROM TABLE @LT_book_suppl. 
endif. 

IF update-znp_i_booksuppl IS NOT INITIAL. 
lt_book_suppl = VALUE #( FOR ls_booksup IN update-znp_i_booksuppl ( 
travel_id = ls_booksup-TravelId 
booking_id = ls_booksup-BookingId 
booking_supplement_id = ls_booksup-BookingSupplementId 
supplement_id = ls_booksup-SupplementId 
price = ls_booksup-Price 
currency_code = ls_booksup-CurrencyCode 
last_changed_at = ls_booksup-LastChangedAt 

) ). 
UPDATE znp_t_booksuppl FROM TABLE @LT_book_suppl. 
ENDIF. 

IF delete-znp_i_booksuppl IS NOT INITIAL. 
lt_book_suppl = VALUE #( FOR ls_del IN delete-znp_i_booksuppl ( 

travel_id = ls_del-TravelId 

booking_id = ls_del-BookingId 

booking_supplement_id = ls_del-BookingSupplementId 

) ). 
DELETE znp_t_booksuppl FROM TABLE @LT_book_suppl. 
ENDIF. 

 

ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

 

. 

 

 For creating an instantiation operation. 

 

 

 

 

Edit operation. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Delete operation,

entry.title: [SAC] Copy Data with Advanced Formula across year is not working
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I have problem with Advanced Formula to copy Data across year. But it is working fine within a year. My code as below:

CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES=OFF
CONFIG.FLIPPING_SIGN_ACCORDING_ACCTYPE=OFF
CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY =[d/COSTCENTER]
CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA=ON

MEMBERSET [d/ACCOUNT] = ("MFG_RM_STD")
MEMBERSET [d/CHANNEL] = ("NO_CHANNEL")
MEMBERSET [d/AUDITTRAIL] = ("INPUT_SAC")
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202401" TO "202512"
MEMBERSET [d/PROFITCENTER] = ("PC001100")
MEMBERSET [d/COMPANY_CODE] = ("001")
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = ("AMT")
MEMBERSET [d/PRODUCT] = "2100000126"

DATA([d/Date]="202501") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date]="202401")

If I changed RESULTLOOKUP to be the same year, then it was working fine, as below

DATA([d/Date]="202502") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date]="202501")

Please suggest,

Thank you

Juthamard

entry.title: 回复： S4HC如何配置采购订单行项目净价字段为只读或隐藏

Hi Qi，

我用了增强 实现了创建采购订单时  净价只读，但是条件类型那边还是能修改，如果条件还能改的话其实还是没有控制住，请问有办法解决吗？

entry.title: Re: ADS Printing failing intermittently in SAP MII
entry.content: Questions:

entry.title: Re: ADS Printing failing intermittently in SAP MII
entry.content: What brand and model of printer are you printing to? Have you assigned a PrinterType and XDC path specfic to the printer type?

entry.title: Re: [SAC] Copy Data with Advanced Formula across year is...
entry.content:
Hi All,

It was my mistakes, I did not provide member for COSTCENTER. I left it blank member, then I could not copy not assigned member across year. So I import CC_NONE to year 2024 and now it is working.

Thank you

Juthamard

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Landscape of the Future
entry.content: @peter_teussink2: See my next blog. I do mention BW here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/i-now-have-sap-s-4hana-embedded-analytics-do-i-still-need-a-data-warehouse/ba-p/13871420

entry.title: Re: Diff beteween 3KEH & FAGL3KEH OKB9
entry.content: Hi, Nice and detailed explanation. Thank you

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content: @nachtaktiv: Let's agree to disagree. I was suprised at how much logic and complex transformations I was able to do with embedded analytics after some practice. I now tell my clients that any operational reporting that can be done in BW can also be done with embedded analytics, as long as you stay within S/4 data. I do agree with you on the topic of dashboarding. Here embedded analytics falls short.

entry.title: Re: How to debug HANA STUDIO
entry.content: Hi, May I know how you resolved issue ? we have a similar issue, we can't open an Open ODS view, even though I can open other BW objects. I didn't try restart yet. can you please share

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content: @MKreitlein: I am curious if you have practical examples of this type of performance issues. So far I was involved in 5 embedded analytics projects, 2 of which were for rather large companies. To my positive suprise, I only encountered performance issues twice. The first time was with financial reports on the large ACDOCA table. This could be solved by improving the query design. The second time was with the SAP standard stack of CDS-views for financial consolidation on top of the ACDOCU table. Issue here is that the queries not only produce output, but the actual financial consolidation is carried out during query runtime, so not a typical reporting situation. But for the rest, no issues with large data volumes or large numbers of concurrent users over a period of 5 years, so surprisingly little performance problems.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano

I am very happy to join the SAP family.
My name is Hassan and I am currently a Human Capital Management Consultant.
From the last +12 years, I have been working as an ORACLE consultant. I am willing to learn and explore SAP as well as the desire to get certified at the same time in order to surpass my knowledge and have better job opportunities in SAP to give such a great opportunity to learn and get a free certificate.
I'm eager to do a free certification try because I believe it will help me learn more and become better at what I do. I know that the world of technology is always changing, and I want to stay up to date with the latest developments. I am grateful for this opportunity and excited about the possibilities ahead. Thank you for considering my application for a free certification attempt.

entry.title: How to perform Master data cleansing activity
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are in the process of cleansing the Material, Vendor, and Customer masters in our S4/HANA platform. I would like to get some expert advice on the points mentioned below.

1. How to categorize the cleansing process (phases)? 

2. What are the most important fields in the Vendor, Customer, and Material masters that we must consider and cleanse?

3.  What are the tables that need to be used to extract the data for the above masters?

4. How to perform the cleansing activities?

5. How to maintain a proper master data creation practice in the future?

Regards,

Kasun

entry.title: Re: Assembly order process - WBS structure
entry.content:
Hi Praveen.

You are correct. The WBS will be created only with WBS elements that have an associated object from the network (header, activities, or sub-activities). In your case, you can link the network header to a level 3 WBS element. However, if your structure contains more than one level 3 WBS element, the entire structure may not be created properly.

Regards 

Marcio Blos.

entry.title: SAP TechEd Virtual: Your SAP Community viewing party | Zurich
entry.content:
Dear Zurich community, 

Join Us for the SAP TechEd Virtual: Your SAP Community Viewing Party!

Get ready for an exciting day of learning and networking at our viewing party in Zurich! Watch SAP TechEd Virtual with fellow SAP enthusiasts, network with experts, and dive into the latest trends in technology.

What to Expect:

  • Live Streaming of SAP TechEd Virtual
  • Workshops from SAP experts in the areas of AI and Application Development
  • Networking Opportunities with local experts from SAP and community members
  • Delicious Food & Drinks to keep you fuelled throughout the event

Agenda:

08:00 - 08:45Check-In & Breakfast
08:45 – 09:00Welcome & Opening
09:00 – 10:30SAP TechEd in 2024 Launch - Where ideas get real
10:30 – 11:00Keynote recap + Community Welcome
11:00 - 11:30Coffee Break
11:30 - 12:30Application Development Track*
Introduction to SAP Build Portfolio and Use Cases

AI Track*
Introduction to SAP Business AI Portfolio and Use Cases 
12:30 - 13:30Lunch
13:30 - 17:00

Application Development Track*
Automate your Business through SAP Build

AI Track*
Generative Al-powered development with Joule copilot

17:00 - 18:00Apéro

*During the registration we’ll ask you to choose between the Workshop Tracks for “Artificial Intelligence” and “App Development”.

Don’t miss out on this fantastic opportunity to learn, share ideas, and grow your SAP skills in a vibrant community atmosphere.

Register by Friday, October 4, 2024 to secure your spot!

We can’t wait to see you at our new SAP Experience Center and make this event a memorable experience together!

SAP Switzerland

***Valid registration via this link only.***

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content: @Martin_Kuma: Yes, you are right. There are solutions for storing historical data or non-SAP data in S/4 itself, bringing it within reach of embedded analytics. Options are Z-tables or embedded BW. Both options are not very popular. Z-tables go against the "keep the core clean" principle usually applied in S/4 implementations. Embedded BW even has me worried about performance. S/4HANA is quite robust and insensitive to performance issues, but bringing another system into the system may be one step too far.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Finance Balance Carryforward technical tip
entry.content:
Hello
We need to Modify/Insert Porfit Center and Partner Profit Center during run time of FAGLGVTR
We want to use Method transfer_mod Of Badi BADI_GVTR_DERIVE_FIELDS. We have implemented the Badi successfully. 
Unfortunately program run does not call the transfer_mod. thus not break Method when break-point set. Do you know why?


Nb. The first Method of the Badi is called successfully thus indcates Badi is implemented and there should be no condition on method call. 

Thanks Kim Brandt

entry.title: Re: Profit Center per customer
entry.content: Hi Priyank do you mind sharing which BADI did you use to achieve this ?

entry.title: Re: How to get data sent from Gmail in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello kohei1sada - 

Step 1: Obtain the Gmail server details. If you're using Gmail, the host address should be smtp.gmail.com.

Step 2: Get the authorization credentials from Gmail. Follow the instructions in this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/setting-up-mail-in-sap-cpi/ba-p/13576752#:~:text=Setting%20up%20Mail%20in%20SAP%20CPI%201%20Download,Add%20GMAIL%20Certificate%20with%20custom%20name.%20More%20items

Step 3: The Mail Sender adapter has a connection tab. Configure the address details there. Set the Proxy type to "Internet" and provide your credentials.

Step 4: In the tab next to Connection, called "Processing," enter the folder name (e.g., "INBOX" or any specific folder). Step 5: In the Selection dropdown box, choose the relevant functionality, such as "Unread emails." After processing, decide the action (e.g., whether to delete the email).

 

Following these steps will help you achieve your goal.

 

Regards,

Karthik Arjun,

SAP Full-Stack Development

entry.title: Re: Need to prevent duplicate values in WWI report
entry.content:
hello

a "quick and dirty" option (and from my perspective the "best practise") is to use an additional rating in data record and generation variant.

E.g. all data record are currently having usage "open/GB". If you define a new rating "X" (value to be defined by you) the business can add usage "X + GB" to the data record which should be displayed in Report. And the "X" must be present in generation variant having highest priority. Anthing else might be tricky.

C.B.

entry.title: Can we add extra column in attachment tab of opportunity?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to add extra column in attachment tab where sales employees can update value withing the column of opportunity in C4C. Is it possible? If yes please suggest me the steps to do so. Find below snap where i want to column/fields.

 

 

Thank you,

Dhaval Solanki

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements and SAP CAP: How to make a field editable depending on condition in Object Page
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a SAP CAP application using Fiori elements for UI, and Im struggling trying to make a field editable on condition in my annotations.cds.

I have this field, and I would like to make it editable depending on status, which is a field from the same entity:

    UI.FieldGroup #1 : { 
        $Type : 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [         
           {
                $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : description,
            },
        ]
   }

 The only thing I found is adding this sentence: 

![@Common.FieldControl]: #ReadOnly
 
But it makes it non editable, so Im trying to make an edm.json condition for the readonly annotation, but not working. 
Is there are any workaround or new approach for this? I think this should be easier to do, so maybe Im focusing bad.
Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: oModel.create is not a function
entry.content:
Hello chaudhary777 - 

Could you refer the below code and blogs. I hope it will guide you some direction. 

 

let oModel = this.getView().getModel();
let oBinding = oModel.bindList("/ENTITY");
oBinding .create({
    "<YOUR ACTION>"
});

 

Reference: 

Step 6: Create and Edit - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK (ondemand.com)

Implementing CRUD Operations in OData v4 - SAP Community

Regards,

Karthik Arjun

SAP Full-Stack Development

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting in Central Finance
entry.content: Nice Blog...thanks for sharing

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning for Expenses based on Income ratio for t...
entry.content:
Hi @SarauShen , this is what we want to happen. The problem is that Sales column is not really a measure in the model. That is a derived value based on the "Sales GL Account per month for a store". We are able to subtotal & bring this sales value. The problem that happens is we need the expense values divided by the "Sales GL Account per month for a store". Ill elaborate the same below: -

Please consider the below data set that is present in the model.

GLStoreMonth.YearAmount
ElectricityA11.2024100
RentA11.2024150
WarehouseA11.2024200
SalesA11.2024500
ElectricityB11.2024150
RentB11.2024225
WarehouseB11.2024300
SalesB11.2024750
ElectricityA12.2024200
RentA12.2024300
WarehouseA12.2024400
SalesA12.20241000
ElectricityB12.2024300
RentB12.2024450
WarehouseB12.2024600
SalesB12.20241500

 

 

The expected value that should come is seen below in the column called "Expected%"

GLStoreMonth.YearAmountExpected %Explaination of calculation
ElectricityA11.202410020%Electricity GL /Sales GL of Store A for 11.2024
RentA11.202415030%Rent/Sales of Store A for 11.2024
WarehouseA11.202420040%Warehouse/Sales of Store A for 11.2024
SalesA11.2024500100%Sales/Sales of Store A for 11.2024
ElectricityB11.202415020%Electricity/Sales of Store B for 11.2024
RentB11.202422530%Rent/Sales of Store B for 11.2024
WarehouseB11.202430040%Warehouse/Sales of Store B for 11.2024
SalesB11.2024750100%Sales/Sales of Store B for 11.2024
ElectricityA12.202420020%Electricity/Sales of Store A for 12.2024
RentA12.202430030%Rent/Sales of Store A for 12.2024
WarehouseA12.202440040%Warehouse/Sales of Store A for 12.2024
SalesA12.20241000100%Sales/Sales of Store A for 12.2024
ElectricityB12.202430020%Electricity/Sales of Store B for 12.2024
RentB12.202445030%Rent/Sales of Store B for 12.2024
WarehouseB12.202460040%Warehouse/Sales of Store B for 12.2024
SalesB12.20241500100%Sales/Sales of Store B for 12.2024

 

We are unable to achieve the % calculation seen in the column "Expected %".

We do not want to hardcode the formula of Electriticity GL/Sales GL in the formula for public dimension of GL Account. Since there are nearly 500 such expense GL codes. Since hierarchies in the GL account wont work if the formulas are hardcoded (for e.g. the hierarchy of Indirect expenses has 20 expense GL codes tagged to the hierarchy. Hence, if the user sees the value of Indirect expenses, the % should be calculated as sum of all the "20 GL's in indirect expenses"/"Sales GL per store per month). We want the system to pick the value present for each row & divide that by the "Sales GL per store per month.year" 

I appreciate your support on this.

entry.title: Issues creating Hana DB Free tier instance on Azure
entry.content:
Hi, 
I am trying to create an instance of SAP Hana cloud in a sub account I set up in Azure west.
Added the entitlements:

  • hana-free
  • relational-data-lake-free
  • hana-cloud-connection-free
  • Tools

When I tried to create the instance the first time, after hitting create instance the error "Unable to connect" pops up.

Trying the process again results in "An error occurred while attempting to create an instance: provision could not be completed: Service broker error: Service broker hana-cloud failed with: You may only create one SAP HANA Cloud Free Tier instance"

So it appears something was created, but I cannot find it as no instance is displayed in either Hana Cloud Central.

I did delete the sub account, recreated again in Azure and tried again to the same effect.

After that, I did check the same process in AWS East (VA), and was able to instantiate a HANA instance there.
I have subsequently deleted that instance, as my purpose was to develop and test a POC on the west coast of the US.

Any idea of what is going on here? What I can do? Or more pointedly, what could I be doing wrong here?
Is Azure not supported for the Free tier for HANA? If not, oh well.

entry.title: Finite Heuristic not considering Ratio of 1 -Tried the Min/Max and LPQuotacoverage KF
entry.content:
Hi All

I would like Finite Heuristic to consider Source Of Supplies (Location and Production) which have Ratio of 1 and not consider the Ratio of 0.

I understand that Finite Heuristic looks at Demand Fulfilment rather than Quota.

Below is my scenario:

  1. Master Data:
    • Products
      • Prod@LOC A  (Buy from Loc B)
      • Prod@LOC B  (Make in Loc B)
    • Location Source
      • Prod@ LOC A ---- SHip From LOC B  -Lead time 1 day - Location Ratio 1
    • Production Source
      • Prod@ LOC A ---Lead Time 0--- Production Ratio 0
      • Prod@ LOC B---Lead Time 8--- Production Ratio 1

Since Finite Heuristic doesnt consider Ratios unlike the Infinite Heuristic, I have tried to maintain the QUOTA related KFs as follows

  • Prod@ LOC A
    • Quota Arrangement Coverage for Location Source = 1 in all periods
  • Prod@ LOC A
    • MIn/Max Production Sourcing Quota = 0 in all periods
  • Prod@ LOC B
    • Min/Max Production Sourcing Quota = 1 in all periods

When I run the S&OP- Finite Heuristic, it is creating Production Receipts at Prod@ LOC A instead of generating Transport Receipts and doing Production at Prod@ LOC B

Any inputs on what I might be missing and is it possible to force the Finite Heuristic to always consider Source Ratio of 1 just like Infinite Heuristic

 

Thanks

Kumar

I have 3 HANA based Calculation views in sys_bic schema.The synonyms created for the views.

How to create proxy entity based on synonyms?

And each of them including one or many lower HU numbers. I want to remove items in lower HUs to the bigger ones and then delete them. But every time i try to move out item, error with status: 'Hu can not be changed' appear and i can't do nothing. please help me to find out at least what is the reason?

entry.title: SAP CAP: Is there any way to modify UI annotations via nodejs events in CAP?
entry.content:
I want to modify/add the readonly property of some fields based on another table to make them editable/non-editable.

I tried with approaches like:

req.target.elements.description["@readonly"] = true
req.target.elements.description._isReadOnly = true...
 
But none of them working. Also tried to put them in the .before, .on and .after and no luck.
Is it possible to do this like im doing? Or I should change the approach?

entry.title: OData V4 / CDS - $count not implemented
entry.content:
Hello,

We have published a CDS view called z_iledelivdocitem as OData V2 and OData V4 services.

An error is raised when we try to get the number of records with the OData V4 service by calling the following url:

https://xxxx:44300/sap/opu/odata4/sap/cdi_cds/cdi_cds/sap/z_iledelivdocitem/0001/Facts/$count

The error message is :

"error": {
"code": "/IWBEP/CM_V4_RUNTIME/040",
"message": "Provider application did not return count",
"@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory": "Provider_Application_Error",
"innererror": {
"ErrorDetails": {
"@SAP__common.Application": {
"ComponentId": "BW-WHM-DBA-ODA",
"ServiceRepository": "CDI_CDS",
"ServiceId": "Z_ILEDELIVDOCITEM",
"ServiceVersion": "0001"
}
...
}

when we add the $sap-ds-debug=true parameter to the url, the exception raised is :

Exception /IWBEP/CX_V4_RUNTIME occured in Class /IWBEP/CL_V4_RESPONSE_INFO_PRO Method VALIDATE_INT and Line 182

IF ( is_todo_list_request-return-count = abap_true ) AND ( ms_done_list-return-count = abap_false ).
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_v4_runtime
EXPORTING
textid = /iwbep/cx_v4_runtime=>todo_list_return_count
exception_category = /iwbep/cx_gateway=>gcs_excep_categories-provider
http_status_code = /iwbep/cx_gateway=>gcs_http_status_codes-sv_not_implemented.
ENDIF.

$count works fine with the OData V2 service with this url :

https://xxxx:44300/sap/opu/odata/sap/Z_ILEDELIVDOCITEM_CDS/Z_ILEDELIVDOCITEM/$count

SAP documentation indicates that $count is supported for two versions of OData.

$count Option | SAP Help Portal

Help appreciated!

It's great to hear this news! We are also organizing the Turkey Inside Track event on the same date. What topics will you be discussing ?

Hello,

Recently, I’ve been focusing my interest on ALM. It has a very broad scope. Currently, I’m continuing my research in project management and test automation. I also appreciate that it integrates process flows with Signavio. I’d love to connect with anyone who has knowledge about this topic or any questions. If anyone is interested, it would be great to discuss!

entry.title: Re: OData V4 / CDS - $count not implemented

entry.title: Error Message for Posting Date Outside Scheduled Dates in Process Order Confirmation
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I need assistance with configuring our SAP system to display an error message when confirming a process order if the posting date is not within the scheduled start and end dates.

We want to ensure that users cannot confirm a process order if the posting date falls outside the scheduled start and end dates of the order by an error message should be triggered during the confirmation process ( using transaction COR6N) if the posting date is not within the scheduled start and end dates.

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks

SAP S/4HANA MAN Production Planning (PP) SAP Industry Process Framework

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello Everyone!!

The first time I heard about SAP, I was in my second year of university. On that very first day, I decided I wanted to become a consultant because it’s a field that never allows for repetition. It’s an industry that continuously adapts to evolving technology and reinvents itself. SAP contributes to all business processes and pushes you to stay up to date with its innovative solutions. I believe these qualities make it a perfect fit for anyone who doesn’t want to fall into a routine and enjoys learning new things.

I work as an SAP MM consultant in Turkey, but recently I’ve started researching ALM. ALM has many interesting features, but I’m currently focusing on project management and test automation. In the public cloud projects I’m involved in, how can I actively use ALM and ensure that our clients do as well? Anyone who wants to share their knowledge and experiences with me is welcome to reach out or ask any questions they may have! 🙂

SAP Cloud ALM SAP Signavio

entry.title: Unable to Fetch the Redayness Report with RC_COLLECT_ANALYSIS_DATA
entry.content:
Hello,

As per the given note- 2913617, we have implemented the required snotes which can be implementable in our system.

However we are still not getting the report as there is some error coming on.

entry.title: Determination and validation on save in determine draft action "Prepare"
entry.content:
I am developing an ABAP RAP managed app with draft capability for a business object that has Header (Root entity) and Line Items (Child entity).

Line Item table has many different columns (for ex: Column A, B, C, and D), and value of "Column D" is determined by the values given in column A, B and C.

Now If user create 3 Line Items and gives values in all 3 columns (A, B and C) and press "Save" or "Check" button - I need to determine the values of column D and perform validations for all 3 rows as well.

And if error comes for any Line Item, then value of Column D must be determined at least for line items those are without error. for ex: If 2nd line item value is not valid, then error should be shown for 2nd row, but value of Column D determined and shown for 1st and 3rd line items.

I tried to implement it with "Determination on save" and "Validation on save" to include in Draft Determine Action "prepare" but eTag mismatch error occurs as determination saves the values in Draft but validation get failed, hence fiori still has old eTag value.

I was thinking to implement side effect as well in column A,B and C, but the problem is that, then side effect will get triggered many times when user creates multiple rows and fill the values in the columns.

Any suggestion - how can i achieve it?

entry.title: Re: File Upload option not showing in SAP RAP Application
entry.content: Could you check this blog post? Especially "With the latest SAP BTP ABAP 2208 release the RAP framework now supports OData streams.". https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/streams-in-rap-uploading-pdf-excel-and-other-files-in-rap-application/ba-p/13526891

entry.title: Re: RAP additional save
entry.content: Thanks. Is it possible that you pretty print your code please? The best pretty printer is ABAP Cleaner. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to post fixed tax amount in countries...
entry.content:
There is no option to comment on an answer, so it's rather silly I have to post this as an answer to my own question. But that's the UX we have in 2024.

@Ryan-Crosbythanks for a reply! We already have a custom pricing procedure, so that ship has sailed. There is a catch-22 here because in a normal US setup, we have a U... something condition, which is statistical and only acts as a "trigger" for JR.. (or XR.. the difference being XR is used with external tax software). And then whatever is in those JR... conditions it's what is going to accounting. JR.. conditions have special type 1..6 (= "tax jurisdiction").

I thought OK, I'll just add JR1 and put the value in it but that idea ends with missing tax code error. Tax code is maintained in condition records (VK11) and you can't do that with JR1 since it has no sequence assigned.

It's possible to create something like ZR1 I guess, but at this point you begin to wonder what else you might run into and if it's even a good idea.

entry.title: WCM Control Key
entry.content:
Hi All, 

Greetings !

I want to run a cycle of permit to work ( WCM ) on S4 Hana system . I am assigning WCM control key in work order operation but WAP is not getting activated . All WCM related business function is activated . Can anyone please shed some light what configuration is missing ? Is there any other method available to activate WCM ?

Thanking you very much

Best Regards 

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: ABOUT MIGO
entry.content: **MIGO** (Movement In Goods Out) is a transaction code in **SAP** used primarily for goods movements, such as receiving materials, transferring stock, or issuing goods from a warehouse. It is part of the **MM (Materials Management)** module, and it's one of the most commonly used functions for inventory management in SAP. Here’s an overview of what **MIGO** does: ### Key Functions of MIGO: 1. **Goods Receipt (GR)**: - Recording the receipt of goods from a purchase order. - The system updates stock quantities and generates accounting documents automatically. 2. **Goods Issue (GI)**: - Issuing materials from inventory for production, sales, or other processes. 3. **Stock Transfer**: - Moving materials between storage locations, plants, or warehouses. 4. **Return Delivery**: - Recording the return of goods to a vendor due to quality issues or over-delivery. 5. **Cancellation of Goods Movement**: - Reversing incorrect goods movements made during goods receipt, issue, or transfer. 6. **Transfer Posting**: - Changing the status or location of materials within the same plant or warehouse, such as from **unrestricted stock** to **quality inspection stock**. ### Basic Steps to Execute MIGO: 1. **Enter the Transaction**: Type `MIGO` in the SAP command bar. 2. **Select the Document Type**: You can choose between Goods Receipt, Goods Issue, Transfer Posting, etc. 3. **Enter Relevant Data**: Depending on the type of movement (e.g., purchase order number for goods receipt). 4. **Post the Document**: After filling in the details, post the movement, which updates the system's stock levels and generates necessary accounting documents. ### Integration with Other SAP Modules: - **MM (Materials Management)**: MIGO is mainly used in MM for managing goods movements. - **PP (Production Planning)**: For issuing materials to production orders. - **SD (Sales and Distribution)**: In cases of goods issue for sales orders. - **WM (Warehouse Management)**: To reflect physical stock movements in a warehouse.

entry.title: Troubleshooting SAP BTP TPM: Debugging Step 1b and XSLT Transformations
entry.content:
Debugging Errors in Trading Partner Management: A Guide

Debugging errors in Trading Partner Management (TPM) isn’t exactly easy or straightforward. Many errors are often summarized with a simple message:

Message ended with Escalation end event

 

In today’s article, we’ll explore how to troubleshoot an error that occurs during step 1b, which is the second step in the standard process. This step involves the initial extraction of identifiers from the message (based on the message type) and populating headers and properties.

 

This extraction process often causes issues because the error messages generated during the extraction (via XSLT) don’t appear in either the MPL monitoring or B2B monitoring tools.

So, if we want to understand what’s happening and why the message processing is failing, we’ll need to simulate this step using external tools.

To do that, we need two things:

  1. The payload of the incoming message — what is being transformed.
  2. The XSLT transformation script — the tool used for the transformation.
 

The Incoming Message Payload

When a message fails with the aforementioned error, you can find the payload of this message in the B2B monitoring section under “Unassigned Interchanges.”

 

By applying the correct filter, you should be able to open the incoming message. In the “Unassigned Interchange Payload” section, you’ll find the payload data.

 

XSLT Transformation

Obtaining the XSLT transformation script is a bit more complex.

In the Partner Directory, you’ll find one entry for each type of incoming message (e.g., IDoc, AS2, SOAP, etc.). If the incoming message is an IDoc, for example, locate the corresponding record in the Partner Directory and note its Partner Directory ID.

 

The correct entry will contain a binary parameter named EXTRACT_XSLT.

 

Next, use a REST client (such as Postman) to call the standard SAP API for retrieving Binary parameters. You can find the API here: SAP API for Binary Parameters.

The returned data will include all binary parameters from that Partner Directory. Look for the record with:

  1. A PID matching your Process Directory ID.
  2. An ID containing EXTRACT_XSLT.

 

In the VALUE element, you’ll find a Base64-encoded ZIP file containing your XSLT transformation script. Copy the entire content of the VALUE element.

Then, decode it and save it as a ZIP file on your local disk. You can use this handy online tool: Base64 Decode.

Once you’ve saved the ZIP file, extract the XSLT transformation script, and you’ll have everything you need to perform the transformation using any tool you’re comfortable with. Personally, I highly recommend using xsltransform.net, a great tool for this task.

 

Note: Standard scripts contains custom functions setExchangeHeader which tries to set Header. It is obviously not possible in this environment, so comment out those lines.

Disclaimer: This article can be also found on my personal blog - https://medium.com/@vbalko/troubleshooting-sap-btp-tpm-debugging-step-1b-and-xslt-transformations-cdf2596b34ee

entry.title: double consumption
entry.content:
hello 

someone help me with a double consumption problem in migo for the output of raw material precisely in the use of zebra scanner barcode with sap fuori

entry.title: Re: NCo 3.1.5.0 SAP.Middleware.Connector.RfcConfigParamet...
entry.content:
Ok, that looks like an entirely different problem:

"System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could not load file or assembly 'System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager, Version=5.0.0.0"

Can it be, that your .NET Core installation is incomplete or corrupt? The missing file is (or should be) part of the Microsoft .NET Core Runtime.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to post fixed tax amount in countries...
entry.content:
I'd try what you did, manual condition with B calculation type, but in TAX procedure (OBYZ, OBQ3), instead of SD pricing procedure. TAx procedures are per country. Take the one you use in US and pick and add another step with a condition with B.  Then define a tax code with a GL on that step with <manual> condition type.

Another route  - If 3rd party tax soft is in use (vertex, sabrix, basware etc), it might be possible to define manual tax rule there, e.g. applicable to all jurisdictions.

entry.title: Re: iOS Usage Strings not updating in build
entry.content:
It's a real pain but I was able to update the strings manually using the steps in this post. You can use TestFlight to verify the strings are accurately updated before you submit to Apple for review. I did not notice any performance issues.

My app was successfully approved after I did all this, but it really is something SAP should fix as it's clearly a bug.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_jung ,

Thanks for creating the table for easy access to the activities for Week 1 of Devtoberfest 2024—it’s really helpful

I also wanted to give you a heads up that a couple of links in the table are directing to the wrong tutorials:

  1. The link for "Use Trial to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application" leads to the tutorial "Get Ready for UI5-Development on Your Local Machine." The correct link should be: Use Trial to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application.

  2. The link for "Use Trial to Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and Document Information E..." takes users to "Create an SAPUI5 App from the Command Line." The intended link should be: Use Trial to Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and Document Information Extraction.

Thanks again for your work on this! Looking forward to the contest kicking off!

Best,
Francisco.

entry.title: SAP Fiori custom application identification
entry.content:
SAP Fiori custom application identification

In SAP S/4HANA projects, application IDs play a critical role in managing SAP Fiori applications. While standard apps come with unique IDs that make them easy to track, custom and extended apps rarely get the same attention. Failing to record the custom app IDs correctly can lead to project confusion, support delays, and documentation gaps. This post will cover the importance of custom app IDs, the challenges teams face without them, and provide a recommendation for proper identification. I'll suggest a tool for managing app IDs and highlight why accurate tracking is key for long-term project success.

Why custom app IDs matter in SAP S/4HANA projects

SAP Fiori standard apps are easily identifiable by a unique App ID, which helps customers find app documentation, communicate in projects, and locate support information. For the same reasons, projects should keep track of custom and extended application IDs.

Reason 1: Finding and maintaining documentation

A list of custom apps with IDs is already a foundation of technical documentation. Although specifying the requirements and features of custom apps takes time, maintaining an official list of the apps is a quick yet fundamental long-term step. Ultimately, you need a list of what needs documentation.

The app list shows the extent of custom solutions in the project. App IDs help connect these with external docs. For example, if your git repo is named after the app ID, everyone can easily find the source with comments and the history of commit descriptions. Similarly, naming transport packages with ABAP objects lets the team see the app's structure without extra documentation. Same applys for MS Word or PDF documents. Orientating documentation around the app ID will help keep it current.

Reason 2: Communication in Projects

Identifying the applications in scope is crucial. It helps with several challenges. First, users can find the right app when contacting support. Next, project members can easily track the status of apps during development, testing, and documentation. Finally, assigning apps to specific business areas clarifies responsibility throughout the project.

Reason 3: Scope management

The apps enabled in the Fiori Launchpad, whether custom or standard, define the project’s scope. Adding a new app, especially a custom one, can be costly. So it’s crucial to track it. To control scope, have the PM approve app additions to the official list before implementation. This simple step ensures clarity and helps maintain control over the scope.

Proper identification

Out of experience, I recommend using the same convention SAP uses in its Fiori application library for App IDs: the letter and four digits. SAP names standard apps using letters other than Z or Y for. This allows projects to use Z and Y for custom and extended apps. For example, "Z0061" is a clear cue immediately showing users they are dealing with a custom app.

When maintaining a list of applications, the choice of tool is not critical as long as the project stores an official list of app IDs in one accessible location. Projects can accomplish this using a SharePoint Excel spreadsheet, a Confluence page, or a similar tool. If you need to connect your app IDs with other records such as Fiori Launchpad catalogs, roles, test users, usage logs, and change requests, consider using a dedicated tool like Fiori Tracker.

Conclusion

Custom App IDs are key to keeping SAP S/4HANA projects organized. They help avoid confusion, improve documentation, and make it easier to manage project scope and support. Whether you use a basic spreadsheet or a tool like Fiori Tracker, tracking these IDs early on will save time and effort later. Keep it simple, but make sure it's done right.

Greg Malewski

Also appeared on Nype's blog.

entry.title: Setting Up the Crossboarding Process with SAP SuccessFactors Recruitment Management
entry.content:
The Internal Hire process facilitates a smooth transition for current employees into new roles within our organization.

Onboarding for an internal hire begins with the onboarding initiation and concludes when the employee steps into their new position. The process can be initiated through:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
  • An external Applicant Tracking System (ATS)

Importantly, the employee's User ID and Username will remain unchanged during the internal hire.

  • Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process

The enhanced onboarding process for internal hires leverages the functionalities of Centralized Services in Employee Central to improve the backend data validation processes in Onboarding.

Navigate to Onboarding General Settings>Turn on Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process

 

  • Enable Internal Hire Options in Manage Data:

Navigate to Manage Data>Select Onboarding Configuration> Select Yes for Centralized Services in Employee Central for Enhanced Internal Hire Process Enabled

Navigate to Manage Data>Select Onboarding Configuration> Select Yes for enhancedCrossboardingOnSL

 

  • Create a Process Variant:

Navigate to Process Variant Manager>click + Process Variant>Add Process Variant dialog box opens>Select the process variant type from the Process Type dropdown, and provide a unique ID and, a Name for the process flow. and click save.

  • Crossboarding Program

We can define programs to manage crossboarding-related tasks, roles, and responsibilities. We can set up specific and relevant tasks for the organization and determine responsible user groups for the tasks. To perform this activity.

Navigate to Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks>Click on + New Program>Add details and Task list from + Add Task option and save.

 

  • Setting Up a Rule for Selecting the Onboarding Program

Define business rules to select the program based on conditions.

Navigate to Configure Business Rules>Click on Add Rule>Select Select Onboarding Task Configuration from the Onboarding tab>Enter the Rule Name, Rule ID, and select a Start Date>Define the rule as per business requirements>Save

 

  • Set up the Future Manager Job Relationship

To ensure the future manager can complete key onboarding tasks before the internal hire’s start date, it’s important to define their role. This picklist value acts as a clear identifier, making it easy to assign the right permissions and responsibilities to those in the future manager position.

Add a picklist value:

Picklist Center>Search Picklist jobRelType>Click on +(Add)>Enter managers (internal hire) as the External Code, provide the Label as Future Manager, and set the Status to Active.

 

  • Permission Role for Future Manager

Manage Permission Roles>Create New Role>Update details like Name, descriptions, and permissions> Under Grant this role to dropdown, choose Future Manager (Internal Hire) and save.

  • Add Event Reasons

Configure the event reason so it can be applied in the business rule to trigger the internal hire process.

Navigate to Manage Organization, Pay and Job Structures>Create New>Event Reason>Enter the details and save.

 

  • Configure Onboarding Initiation Business Rule

  • Configure a Transfer Event Reason

  • Assign Rule to Job Information

Go to Manage Business Configuration>under HRIS Elements select jobInfo>Scroll down to the trigger rules section>set the Transfer rule for the Event Type as onPostSave.

 

  • Configure Event Reason Picklist

After completing the crossboarding setup, The next step in populating event reasons in recruitment is to add all transfer-related event reasons to a picklist, ensuring they have the same external code as the event reason code in Manage Organization, Pay, and Job Structures.

 

  • Configure Event Reason Field in Recruitment

The next step is to add a picklist field in recruitment, either in the job application or job requisition. Then, assign the necessary permissions, such as allowing R to edit this field during the onboarding or offer stages.

 

  • Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping

The next step is to map the event reason field from Recruitment to Onboarding, allowing the system to automatically pre-populate the event reason value when Onboarding is initiated.

  •  Business Rule to Populate Job Information Based on Position

There are two ways to update the internal hire's job information when initiating crossboarding from recruitment:

  1. Map all job information fields directly from recruitment.
  2. Create a business rule to populate job information using the position details.

After creating the rule, assign it to the position field under job information in Manage Business Configuration.

Note: Please make sure only the context of the “Onboarding” rule is true.

In all scenarios, we need to have the same rule twice. One with all other contexts as true and another one with the “Onboarding” rule context as true.

  • Initiate Crossboarding Using Recruitment

Once the job is posted internally and the Internal Hire has completed all the necessary steps in recruitment. The recruiter needs to select the correct event reason for recruitment and initiate crossboarding using the Initiate Onboarding button under the action menu.

 

  • Complete Review Employee Information Step

Once crossboarding is successfully initiated by recruitment, the candidate will be visible on the home page Onboarding tile or in the Onboarding dashboard.

Click on the candidate’s name and complete the Review Employee Information Step
Under the job information section, you can view the new job information of the internal hire.
You can also click on the “Clock” icon just to see the changes in job information.

You can validate the source as Crossboarding.

Currently, Crossboarding is supported for the below entities only:

JOB_RELATIONS_INFO
PAY_COMPONENT_NON_RECURRING
PAY_COMPONENT_RECURRING
EMP INFO
JOB INFO
COMP INFO

Note: In the above scenario we are only passing the job information.

Conclusion:

The Internal Hire process provides an excellent chance for companies to utilize the skills and expertise of their current employees.

SAP SuccessFactors is a good tool that supports internal hire and crossboarding processes within companies. Its customizable features enable organizations to adapt the internal hire and crossboarding processes to meet their unique needs.

Useful Link:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2970083

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_ONBOARDING/c94ed5fcb5fe4e0281f396556743812c/da569f9782e8458daf687d18e52ea4ab.html

entry.title: Re: CVI Issue - No Log recorded in Tcode MDS_PPO2
entry.content: Hi Rahul were you able to resolve the issue?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to post fixed tax amount in countries...
entry.content: We encountered this FF805 error during our first phase of our S4 go-live in early 2022. The SD consultant for our first SI setup a custom pricing procedure with MWST even though our main business is out of the US, and then it happened because of the missing JRx conditions with UTXJ. We were sort of in the reverse case where we needed regular calculations for the rare case where taxes applied, but the setup fouled up the execution in pricing. What I do recall is spinning in loops on the tax determination documentation and how it's all intended to work because a good chunk of the help/documentation is vague and "Hello World" like - ended up making more progress simply debugging the code and landed in some of the same spots you mentioned in your post.

entry.title: Re: Registered for devtoberfest last year!
entry.content:
Hello Devraj,

you are already in. Check out your user profile, you have already got the "Devtoberfest Participant"-Badge for this year.

entry.title: Re: Set Default Value in RAP Managed App (without draft)
entry.content:
Thank you for your inputs. I tried to follow along the same steps. It works for Create Scenario but one scenario doesn't work in Update.

When I try to edit a record Where Status = 'No' & Expiration Date= 'Some_Value'. And I just empty out the Expiration field and try to save it.

In this case the determination does not trigger to set it to default value, And the record is saved with Expdate = 'empty'. I assume because Status field was not edited at all.

I  have defined validation & determination as follows:

validation ValidateExpdate on save { create; update; field Expdate; }

  • In this method I have set a check If Status = 'Yes' & Expdate IS INITIAL, Raise a error message to user. This makes Expdate Mandatory if Status = 'Yes'.

determination DefaultExpDate on modify { field Status; create; }

  • In this method I am trying to set the Default Value for ExpDate = 'Any_value'.

Please let me know if you have any other input. Thank you.

entry.title: GUI MacOS screen controls
entry.content:
Hi fellow MacOs users!

Ever since I upgraded to Java Gui version 7.80 rev 4 (to start with) the window controls have been missing on my screen. I can go back to 7.70 rev 4 or prior and it works fine - even side by side. I have since installed several  other versions through 7.80 rev 7 and also updated my macOS to now using Sequoia 15.0. This behavior is still the same. This is a small thing, but constantly has me changing the way I work in SAP opposed to other programs.

The attached screen shot shows the two versions running side-by-side and the screen controls highlighted on the older version of the GUI.

Is anyone else facing this issue - I have not seen anything in this community or in Google searches? Does anyone have any thoughts on a fix?

Thank you!!

@RMW 

UI SAP GUI for Java

entry.title: Re: ABAP class/FM/BAPI/IDOC to create/maintain Inbound De...
entry.content: This program (/SCWM/R_DLV_ADJUST) performs read/query operations on Inbound & Outbound delivery that are already created. I am trying to build a solution to create Inbound delivery. Debuggine /SCWM/PRDI transaction seemed a bit too tedious and hence I went with debugging IDOC function module "/SCWM/IDOC_INPUT_IBDLV_SAVEREP". Inside this FM, I found a class used for inbound delivery creation (for delivery replica). The class is "/scwm/cl_mapin_id_savereplica". Further, when I checked the function group of this IDOC FM, there was a wrapper Function Module for creating EWM Inbound Delivery and it used the same class and approach as IDOC. After a lot of experimentations and failures, I found a way for this Function Module to work and create Inbound Delivery in EWM Decentralized system independently. It works for my scenerio.

entry.title: Re: Cannot GET /redirect error in accessing SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content: This worked for me too.Accessing SAP Business Application Studio

entry.title: Re: Cannot GET /redirect error in accessing SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content: This worked for me too. Accessing SAP Business Application Studio

entry.title: Re: Cannot GET /redirect error in accessing SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content: This worked for me too. By accessing SAP Business Application Studio from SAP BTP Cockpit > trial subaccount > Services > Instances and Subscriptions > SAP Business Application Studio (under Application section)

entry.content: Good execise
